text:
Agile methodology is a popular approach to software development. Work is divided into short cycles called **sprints**, which usually last one to four weeks. Each sprint focuses on completing a set of tasks from the **backlog**, a list of all required features and fixes. The team prioritises these tasks using **user stories**, which describe features from a user's perspective.

In agile, development is **iterative**, meaning software is built, tested, and improved through repeated cycles. Every day, the team holds a quick meeting called a **standup**. During standups, each member shares what they did yesterday, what they will do today, and any obstacles they face. This process helps teams adapt to changes quickly and deliver value to customers regularly.
